_metadata.description: Regional Intelligence Configuration for Flood Vulnerability ML Model â€” EXPANDED
_metadata.changelog: v2.1.0 â€” Added 35 new cities: 15 additional Indian cities and 20 global cities across Southeast Asia, Middle East, Central Asia, Europe, Africa, Americas, and Oceania.
_metadata.architecture_note: This file serves as the STATIC REGIONAL INTELLIGENCE LAYER. Each city profile captures long-term environmental, infrastructure, and governance conditions on a 0-100 scale. Live weather data from APIs such as Open-Meteo (https://open-meteo.com) will be combined with these profiles at inference time to generate DYNAMIC flood risk scores. Formula: Static Vulnerability Profile + Live Rainfall/Weather Signal + ML Pipeline = Dynamic Flood Risk Score.
_metadata.feature_scale: All ML feature values are scaled 0-100 to match model inference expectations
_metadata.data_sources: Values derived from IPCC regional reports, UN-Habitat city profiles, World Resources Institute Aqueduct, Asian Development Bank flood risk assessments, European Environment Agency, FEMA, NDMA India, Kerala SDMA, OCHA, EM-DAT International Disaster Database

notable_flood_events: 2018 Kerala floods (worst in a century, 483 deaths, 1.4M displaced)
2019 Kerala floods
Annual monsoon flooding of Ernakulam districts
regions.region_notes: Kochi is Kerala's commercial hub, built across a network of islands, backwaters, and reclaimed land. The 2018 megaflood submerged large parts of the city. Rapid urbanization has consumed wetlands that historically acted as natural buffers. The Periyar river system and Vembanad Lake create compounding flood dynamics during intense monsoon events.

notable_flood_events: 2018 Kerala floods â€” Chalakudy river basin overflow
Cheruthoni dam spillway release (2018) caused catastrophic downstream flooding
regions.region_notes: Thrissur district was among the most severely affected during the 2018 Kerala floods. The Chalakudy river, one of Kerala's most flood-prone rivers, runs through the district. The Idamalayar and Bhoothathankettu dam systems are critical flood buffers. Deforestation in the Western Ghats catchment directly intensifies downstream flood risk.

notable_flood_events: 2018 Kerala floods â€” Kuttanad below sea-level region submerged for weeks
1924 Great Flood of Kerala historical precedent
Annual kharif season waterlogging in Kuttanad
regions.region_notes: Alappuzha (Alleppey) contains Kuttanad, known as the 'Rice Bowl of Kerala' and one of the few places on Earth where farming occurs below sea level. The district is a network of canals, lakes, and paddy fields. During the 2018 floods, Kuttanad remained submerged for over three weeks. Pump-drained polders are the primary flood control mechanism â€” a system under increasing stress.

notable_flood_events: 2018 Kerala floods â€” Pampa river catastrophic overflow, Sabarimala pilgrimage route severed
2019 floods â€” repeated inundation of Ranni and Kozhencherry taluks
regions.region_notes: Pathanamthitta district sits at the foothills of the Western Ghats with the Pampa and Manimala rivers flowing through it. The 2018 floods were particularly severe here â€” the Pampa river at Kozhencherry reached its highest recorded level. Rapid deforestation in the upper catchments of the Western Ghats and encroachments on riverbanks have dramatically increased flood intensity in recent decades.

notable_flood_events: 2018 Kerala floods â€” landslide-flood combination events
2019 Puthumala landslide triggered by flooding
2024 Mundakkai-Chooralmala catastrophic landslide (most deadly in Kerala history, 400+ deaths)
regions.region_notes: Wayanad is a highland district on the Western Ghats plateau with exceptional biodiversity but extreme landslide-flood coupling risk. Deforestation for plantation agriculture (tea, coffee, cardamom) has destabilised slopes. The 2024 Mundakkai disaster â€” a combination of extreme rainfall and slope failure â€” killed over 400 people, becoming Kerala's deadliest natural disaster in recent memory.

notable_flood_events: 2018 Kerala floods â€” Chaliyar river overflow, Kalpetta and Sulthan Bathery hillside flooding
Annual Kallai and Kadalundi river bank flooding
regions.region_notes: Kozhikode (Calicut) is a historic port city on Kerala's Malabar Coast. The Chaliyar, Kadalundi, and Kallai rivers drain Western Ghats catchments through the district. Coastal erosion and monsoon intensification create dual-front vulnerability.

notable_flood_events: 2018 Kerala floods â€” Kadalundi river overflow, Nilambur and Ponnani severely affected
2019 Kerala floods â€” widespread flooding and landslides in hilly eastern regions
Seasonal Bharathapuzha and Kadalundi basin flooding during Southwest Monsoon
regions.region_notes: Malappuram is one of Kerala's most densely populated districts spanning coastal plains, river basins, and Western Ghats foothills. The district is highly exposed to intense monsoon rainfall, river overflow, hillside erosion, and flash flooding.

notable_flood_events: 2018 Kerala floods â€” Valapattanam and Mahe river flooding
2019 landslide-flood events in eastern highland areas
Annual riverbank flooding in low-lying taluks
regions.region_notes: Kannur district in northern Kerala sits at the convergence of coastal lowlands, river deltas, and Western Ghats foothills. The Valapattanam and Mahe rivers overflow regularly during the southwest monsoon. Northern Malabar sees both coastal erosion and inland flood risk. Cashew and coconut plantation areas on slopes face landslide risk during extreme rain.

notable_flood_events: 2018 Kerala floods â€” Chandragiri and Payaswini river overflow
2019 floods with significant infrastructure damage
Endosulfan-contaminated region adds compound health-flood risk
regions.region_notes: Kasaragod, Kerala's northernmost district, is bordered by Karnataka and the Arabian Sea. The Chandragiri, Payaswini, and Kariangode rivers drain Western Ghats slopes through the district. The district also carries long-term contamination legacy from aerial endosulfan spraying, making flood events compound environmental disasters. Coastal erosion is severe along the Kasaragod shoreline.

notable_flood_events: 2018 Kerala floods â€” Periyar river and Cheruthoni dam spillway crisis
2021 Rajamala landslide (Munnar area) triggered by extreme rainfall
Frequent landslides in tea/cardamom estate zones of Western Ghats
regions.region_notes: Idukki is a mountainous district entirely within the Western Ghats, housing Kerala's largest arch dam (Idukki Dam). The district is the source of the Periyar river, which drains into Kochi's backwaters. Tea and cardamom plantations on steep slopes are highly landslide-prone. The 2018 Cheruthoni dam spillway release caused catastrophic downstream flooding. Climate change is intensifying extreme precipitation events in this high-altitude catchment.

notable_flood_events: 2018 Kerala floods â€” Kallada river overflow and Ashtamudi Lake flooding
2019 floods affecting low-lying coastal areas
Frequent coastal erosion events along Kollam beach
regions.region_notes: Kollam is a coastal port city in southern Kerala with a long coastline on the Arabian Sea and the Ashtamudi Lake â€” the second-largest wetland in Kerala. The Kallada river system drains into this estuary. The district faces both river flooding and coastal inundation from wave action and storm surge. Cashew processing industrial areas in low-lying zones face flood damage risk each monsoon season.

notable_flood_events: 2018 Kerala floods â€” Neyyar and Karamana river flooding, airport area inundated
2021 Ockhi cyclone-related coastal surge (2017 precursor)
Annual waterlogging of low-lying areas during southwest monsoon
regions.region_notes: Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala's capital, sits at the southernmost tip of the state along the Arabian Sea coast. The Karamana, Neyyar, and Killiyar rivers drain through the city. The densely built capital has poor drainage in low-lying areas. The international airport sits in a flood-prone zone. Coastal erosion has intensified along the Shanghumugham and Vizhinjam coastlines. The new Vizhinjam port development adds new dimensions to coastal flood risk.

notable_flood_events: 2018 Kerala floods â€” Bharathapuzha (Nila) river overflow, Malampuzha dam discharge
Frequent crop damage flooding in Palakkad plains (Kerala's rice bowl)
2019 floods affecting Chittur and Mannarkkad taluks
regions.region_notes: Palakkad district lies in the famous Palakkad Gap â€” a natural break in the Western Ghats â€” making it climatically distinct from the rest of Kerala. The Bharathapuzha (Nila River) is Kerala's second longest river and is severely stressed by sand mining and encroachment. The Malampuzha reservoir serves agriculture but creates downstream flood risk during spillway releases. The region's flat plains flood extensively during intense rainfall events.

notable_flood_events: 2005 Mumbai floods â€” 944mm in 24 hours, 1,094 deaths
2017 Mumbai floods â€” 298mm in 12 hours, city paralysed
Annual monsoon flooding of low-lying suburbs
regions.region_notes: Mumbai receives among the world's heaviest monsoon rainfall. The city is built on reclaimed land with a dense informal settlement network. Its drainage infrastructure, designed for colonial-era capacity, is severely stressed by rapid urbanization and population growth.

notable_flood_events: 2019 Pune floods â€” Khadakwasla dam release caused catastrophic Mutha river flooding, 25+ deaths
2021 Pune district floods â€” 50+ deaths in Mahabaleshwar upstream
Annual Mula-Mutha river flooding in low-lying city areas
regions.region_notes: Pune sits on the Deccan Plateau at the junction of the Mula and Mutha rivers. Rapid IT-driven expansion has consumed floodplains extensively. The Khadakwasla dam system provides water supply but creates acute downstream flood risk when spillways open during heavy monsoon. Hillside areas around Pune (Katraj, Warje) face landslide risk. The 2019 dam release flooded major city arteries.

notable_flood_events: 2022 Bengaluru floods â€” IT corridors including Whitefield and Sarjapur submerged, Rs 300 crore damage
2017 flooding of Bellandur lake fire zone
Annual flooding of Outer Ring Road low-lying stretches
regions.region_notes: Bengaluru, India's IT capital, has destroyed over 90% of its historical lakes through encroachment and converted natural drainage valleys into real-estate corridors. The city's rapid and unplanned expansion has created urban flood hotspots in Whitefield, Marathahalli, and Electronic City. The Bellandur and Varthur lake systems â€” severely polluted and encroached â€” are at the centre of the flood crisis.

notable_flood_events: 2019 Patna urban floods â€” city centre submerged, 73 deaths statewide
Annual Bihar floods affecting 10-15M people each monsoon season
2017 Bihar floods â€” 500+ deaths, 8M displaced
regions.region_notes: Patna, Bihar's capital, sits on the southern bank of the Ganga river which is one of the world's largest river systems by discharge. Bihar is India's most flood-prone state â€” nearly 76% of its land north of the Ganga is flood-affected. The 2019 urban floods exposed catastrophic gaps in Patna's storm drain capacity. The Kosi, Gandak, Bagmati, and Mahananda rivers compound Ganga basin flooding.

notable_flood_events: 1999 Odisha super cyclone â€” 10,000 deaths (among India's worst natural disasters)
2013 Cyclone Phailin â€” 1M evacuated, Odisha coast devastated
Annual Mahanadi river flooding of interior Odisha districts
regions.region_notes: Bhubaneswar is Odisha's capital and sits near the Mahanadi river delta. Odisha is among India's most cyclone-exposed states â€” the Bay of Bengal generates intense cyclones that make landfall on the Odisha coast with devastating regularity. Post-1999 cyclone, Odisha has built one of India's best disaster management systems, significantly reducing mortality though physical exposure remains extreme.

notable_flood_events: 2009 cyclone-linked Krishna river flooding â€” 200+ deaths
2020 Krishna river floods â€” worst in decades, Prakasam Barrage discharged record flows
2021 flooding of low-lying areas during heavy monsoon
regions.region_notes: Vijayawada sits at the head of the Krishna River delta on the Bay of Bengal coastal plain. The Krishna river's Prakasam Barrage is a critical flood management structure â€” when it releases, low-lying areas of the city flood rapidly. The delta region is also exposed to Bay of Bengal cyclones. Rapid urbanization of floodplain areas has made Vijayawada among Andhra Pradesh's most flood-vulnerable cities.

notable_flood_events: 2006 Surat floods â€” Ukai dam discharge, 90% of city submerged, 150 deaths
2013 Surat urban floods from Tapi river overflow
2017 extreme Ukai dam release flooding
regions.region_notes: Surat, India's diamond and textile capital, sits at the mouth of the Tapi river. The Ukai reservoir upstream is India's second largest. When Ukai releases water during extreme monsoon years, Surat floods rapidly â€” a problem compounded by the city's low elevation relative to the river. The 2006 flood, where 90% of the city submerged, transformed Surat's disaster management approach into one of India's more resilient urban systems.

notable_flood_events: 2023 Yamuna river unprecedented flooding â€” highest level in 45 years, 26,000 evacuated
2013 Yamuna flooding of low-lying areas
Annual monsoon waterlogging of underpass and low-lying colonies
regions.region_notes: Delhi sits on the Yamuna river floodplain. The Yamuna's floodplain has been massively encroached by informal settlements and even formal infrastructure. In 2023, the river reached its highest level in 45 years, flooding large parts of the city including near the Civil Lines district. Upstream Hathnikund Barrage releases combined with heavy local rainfall create acute flood risk. Delhi's drainage infrastructure is overwhelmed by even moderate monsoon events.

notable_flood_events: 2015 Chennai floods â€” worst in 100 years, 500 deaths, $3B damage
2021 cyclone Nivar-linked flooding
2023 Cyclone Michaung â€” city submerged
regions.region_notes: Chennai experiences both northeast monsoon flooding and cyclone-driven storm surges. The Adyar and Cooum rivers, historically bounded by floodplains, are now encroached by dense development. The 2015 disaster revealed catastrophic failures in water body preservation, floodplain regulation, and drainage maintenance. Wetland destruction for IT corridor development has eliminated natural flood storage.

notable_flood_events: 2021 Cyclone Yaas â€” storm surge flooding
Annual monsoon waterlogging of low-lying wards
2017 flooding of eastern fringes from Amphan-linked events
regions.region_notes: Kolkata sits on the Hooghly River delta with vast areas at or below mean sea level. The city is surrounded by the Sundarbans delta, one of the world's most cyclone-exposed coastlines. Ageing colonial-era sewer and drainage infrastructure, rapid eastward expansion onto wetlands, and intensifying Bay of Bengal cyclones make Kolkata increasingly flood-vulnerable.

notable_flood_events: Annual Brahmaputra river flooding â€” Assam floods affect 5M+ people every year
2022 Assam floods â€” 200 deaths, 5.4M affected
2012 Brahmaputra embankment breaches
regions.region_notes: Guwahati is the gateway to Northeast India, located on the Brahmaputra river's southern bank. The Brahmaputra is one of the world's most flood-powerful rivers â€” carrying the highest sediment load globally. Annual flooding is a near-certainty due to Himalayan snowmelt, monsoon rainfall, and heavy siltation.

notable_flood_events: 2020 Hyderabad floods â€” 50 deaths, 200,000 displaced, $600M damage
2000 Musi river floods
2016 Durgam Cheruvu area inundation
regions.region_notes: Hyderabad's rapid IT-sector-driven expansion has consumed hundreds of historical lakes (cheruvu) that served as natural flood retention basins. The Musi River bisects the city, and encroachment on its flood plain is extensive. Heavy but concentrated monsoon events cause catastrophic urban flash flooding.

notable_flood_events: 2020 New Year floods â€” 60,000 displaced
2013 Jakarta floods â€” 20 deaths, $3B damage
Annual wet-season flooding affecting millions
regions.region_notes: Jakarta sits on low-lying coastal land with ground subsidence up to 25cm/year. Over 40% of the city is below sea level. 13 rivers intersect the urban area. The capital is being relocated to Nusantara in Borneo.

notable_flood_events: 2011 Thailand floods â€” worst in 50 years, $45B damage, 815 deaths
Annual Chao Phraya river flood season
regions.region_notes: Bangkok is built on the Chao Phraya delta with land subsidence of 2-3cm/year. The 2011 floods submerged much of the metropolitan area for months.

notable_flood_events: 2009 Typhoon Ondoy â€” 80% of Metro Manila submerged
2013 Typhoon Haiyan (Yolanda) coastal surge
Annual typhoon season (20 typhoons/year average)
regions.region_notes: Manila combines tropical storm intensity, poor drainage, informal waterway encroachments. Average 20 typhoons per year hit the Philippines.

notable_flood_events: 1998 Bangladesh floods â€” 68% of country submerged, 1,000 deaths
2017 floods â€” 8M affected
Annual monsoon inundation of low-lying zones
regions.region_notes: Bangladesh is a delta nation â€” 80% floodplain. Climate models predict 17% of land will be submerged by 2050. Dhaka surrounded by 4 major rivers.

notable_flood_events: Annual urban tidal flooding worsening each decade
2016 severe urban flash floods
Mekong Delta flooding regularly displaces millions
regions.region_notes: Ho Chi Minh City (Saigon) lies on the Saigon River delta, just 1-2m above sea level. Tidal flooding has increased dramatically due to sea-level rise and subsidence from groundwater extraction.

notable_flood_events: 2021-22 Malaysia floods â€” worst in decades, 70 deaths, 125,000 evacuated
Annual flash flooding in Klang valley
regions.region_notes: Kuala Lumpur sits at the Klang-Gombak river confluence. The SMART tunnel is a world-first dual flood-road system. Rapid highland deforestation intensifies flash flood risk.

notable_flood_events: 2019 Typhoon Hagibis â€” 100+ deaths, 70,000 homes damaged
Historical Kanto floods pre-MAOUDC construction
regions.region_notes: Tokyo built the MAOUDC â€” world's largest underground flood channel. Despite typhoon exposure, engineering excellence and governance keep impacts minimal.

notable_flood_events: 2008 Cyclone Nargis â€” 138,000 deaths (Irrawaddy delta)
2015 Myanmar floods â€” 1M displaced
Annual monsoon flooding of Irrawaddy delta lowlands
regions.region_notes: Yangon sits on the Irrawaddy delta, one of Asia's most flood-prone deltas. Cyclone Nargis remains one of the deadliest natural disasters in Asian history. Weak governance and infrastructure compound physical exposure significantly.

notable_flood_events: 2020 Karachi urban floods â€” 90 deaths, infrastructure collapse
2022 Pakistan super-floods â€” one-third of country submerged, 1,739 deaths
regions.region_notes: Karachi is a megacity with severely inadequate stormwater infrastructure. Flash floods from intense monsoon events overwhelm the city rapidly. The 2022 Pakistan floods were described as a 'climate catastrophe' by the UN.

notable_flood_events: 2016 Sri Lanka floods â€” 200 deaths, 1M displaced
2017 southwestern floods and mudslides â€” 200+ deaths
Annual May-June monsoon flooding of Kelani river basin
regions.region_notes: Colombo, Sri Lanka's commercial capital, sits on a coastal plain crossed by the Kelani River, one of the island's most flood-prone rivers. The city has extensive wetland areas â€” including the Beira Lake system and Muthurajawela marsh â€” that serve as natural flood buffers but are under intense urbanization pressure. Flood risk intensifies with La Nina-driven southwest monsoon years. Sea-level rise threatens low-lying coastal neighborhoods.

notable_flood_events: Annual Bagmati river flooding during monsoon season
2014 Jure landslide â€” Sunkoshi river damming event
2021 Nepal floods â€” 200+ deaths, widespread displacement across Terai
regions.region_notes: Kathmandu sits in a valley basin surrounded by Himalayan foothills. The Bagmati, Bishnumati, and Manohara rivers drain the valley. Rapid urban encroachment on river corridors has severely narrowed floodways. The valley's bowl-like topography traps floodwater. Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) from Himalayan glaciers represent a growing upstream threat. Monsoon landslides are frequent on steep periurban slopes.

notable_flood_events: 2010 Pakistan floods â€” deadliest in Pakistan history, 2,000 deaths, 20M displaced
2014 Lahore urban floods â€” flash flooding from monsoon bursts
2022 Pakistan super-floods â€” Indus and Ravi basins catastrophically inundated
regions.region_notes: Lahore sits on the Ravi river floodplain in Punjab province. Pakistan's 2010 floods â€” driven by unprecedented monsoon rainfall â€” submerged one-fifth of the entire country and remain one of the world's costliest natural disasters. The Ravi river is now heavily diminished by upstream Indian diversion but still poses flood risk during extreme events. Lahore's rapid suburban expansion has consumed natural drainage corridors.

notable_flood_events: 2024 Afghanistan flash floods â€” 400+ deaths, worst in decades
2022 eastern Afghanistan floods â€” 150+ deaths
Annual spring snowmelt flooding of Kabul river
regions.region_notes: Kabul sits in a mountain-ringed basin at 1,800m elevation. The Kabul River and its tributaries flash-flood during spring snowmelt and intense convective storms. Decades of conflict have destroyed drainage and flood infrastructure. Massive informal settlement growth on river margins has created extreme exposure. Climate change is intensifying precipitation extremes even in this semi-arid zone. Governance capacity for disaster response is critically limited.

notable_flood_events: 2019 Iran floods â€” deadliest in decades, 70+ deaths, $2.5B damage
2018 Tehran flash flooding â€” urban waterways overwhelmed
Recurring spring flash floods in northern Tehran foothills
regions.region_notes: Tehran is built on alluvial fans at the base of the Alborz Mountains, which creates extreme flash flood risk from short-duration intense storms. The northern wealthy districts sit on mountain slopes with high landslide risk. Qanat water channels and seasonal streams (jub) become raging torrents during precipitation events. The city's sprawl has consumed natural drainage corridors, and informal settlements on northern slopes face compound hazard.

notable_flood_events: 1953 North Sea Flood â€” 1,835 deaths (pre-Delta Works)
Delta Works completed 1997 â€” flood risk dramatically reduced since
regions.region_notes: Amsterdam exemplifies world-class water management. 26% of Netherlands below sea level. Delta Works and extensive polder/pump network make flooding rare despite geographic vulnerability.

notable_flood_events: 1953 East Coast floods â€” 307 deaths in UK
2007 Tewkesbury floods (Thames tributaries)
Thames Barrier activated 200+ times since 1982
regions.region_notes: London protected by Thames Barrier since 1982. Ageing Victorian sewers and climate intensification remain growing concerns. Barrier design lifespan extends to 2070 with upgrades.

notable_flood_events: 2019 acqua alta â€” worst flooding in 50 years, 187cm water level
Annual Acqua Alta events increasing in frequency
MOSE barrier became operational 2020
regions.region_notes: Venice is built on 118 islands in the Venetian Lagoon and faces existential threat from sea-level rise combined with land subsidence (23cm in 20th century). The MOSE flood barrier system became operational in 2020.

notable_flood_events: 1962 Hamburg storm surge â€” 315 deaths (transformed German flood policy)
2021 Ahr Valley floods â€” 184 deaths, worst German flooding in 50 years (nearby region)
regions.region_notes: Hamburg sits at the Elbe estuary and has invested heavily in flood defences post-1962. The HafenCity development is built on elevated plinths as a flood-adaptive urban design model.

notable_flood_events: 2002 Danube floods â€” record water levels
2013 Danube floods â€” 890cm, highest recorded level
Regular spring snowmelt flooding
regions.region_notes: Budapest straddles the Danube, one of Europe's most flood-prone rivers. Snowmelt from the Alps combined with heavy spring rainfall creates predictable but severe flood seasons.

notable_flood_events: 2023 Lisbon flash floods
Annual Tagus river flooding of low-lying areas
1967 Lisbon floods â€” 700 deaths (historic)
regions.region_notes: Lisbon sits at the Tagus River estuary. Atlantic storm systems drive intense winter rainfall events causing flash flooding in hillside neighbourhoods.

notable_flood_events: 2010 Vistula floods â€” worst since 1960s, extensive embankment pressure
1997 Millennium Flood (downstream Odra river â€” affected region)
Regular spring ice-jam flooding of Vistula
regions.region_notes: Warsaw sits on the Vistula river, Poland's longest and most flood-prone river. The city has extensive flood embankments but remains vulnerable to extreme snowmelt-rain compound events. Historic floodplain areas (Praga district) remain at risk. Climate change is altering the snowmelt timing and rainfall patterns, creating new flood windows outside traditional spring seasons.

notable_flood_events: 2012 Nigeria floods â€” worst in 40 years, 2.1M displaced
Annual Lagos flooding displaces 200,000+ each year
2011 Kano floods, 2017 Benue floods
regions.region_notes: Lagos is Africa's largest city, built on a network of islands and lagoons with vast areas of informal settlement on flood-prone land. Inadequate drainage, rapid unplanned urbanization, and intense tropical rainfall make flooding a near-annual catastrophe for millions.

notable_flood_events: 2020 Sudan floods â€” worst in 100 years, 875,000 affected, Nile reached 17.57m (record)
2022 Sudan floods â€” 100+ deaths, 300,000 displaced
regions.region_notes: Khartoum sits at the confluence of the Blue and White Nile rivers. Catastrophic flooding in 2020 reached the highest Nile levels in a century.

notable_flood_events: 2015 Accra floods â€” 150+ deaths, fuel station explosion
Annual June-July flooding of Odaw river basin
2022 Accra floods
regions.region_notes: Accra's rapid growth has overwhelmed drainage infrastructure. The Odaw river and its tributaries are heavily encroached. Poor solid waste management chokes drains.

notable_flood_events: 2019 Cyclone Idai â€” 1,300 deaths, 90% of Beira destroyed
2019 Cyclone Kenneth (weeks later)
2000 Mozambique floods â€” 700 deaths
regions.region_notes: Beira, Mozambique's second-largest city, was described as 'the most flood-prone city in Africa.' It sits just 1-3m above sea level. Cyclone Idai in 2019 essentially destroyed the city.

notable_flood_events: 2011 floods â€” 20 deaths
2014 Dar es Salaam floods â€” 30 deaths
Annual flooding of Msimbazi River basin
regions.region_notes: Dar es Salaam is one of the world's fastest-growing cities, with vast informal settlements on flood-prone coastal and riverine lowlands. The Msimbazi river regularly floods.

notable_flood_events: 2020 Cairo flash floods â€” rare but deadly desert flash flood event
2021 Alexandria floods
Nile Delta sea-level rise is a slow-onset disaster
regions.region_notes: Cairo's flood risk is unusual â€” the Nile is heavily managed by the Aswan High Dam, but climate change is producing unprecedented desert flash floods from intense convective storms.

notable_flood_events: 2016 Luanda floods
2019 Luanda floods â€” 30 deaths, 5,000 displaced
Annual mudika (shantytown) flooding events
regions.region_notes: Luanda is one of Africa's most expensive cities with extreme inequality â€” vast informal musseques (shanty settlements) on steep hillsides face deadly mudslide-flood combinations each rainy season.

notable_flood_events: 2023 DRC floods â€” 400+ deaths, worst in years
2022 Kinshasa flooding â€” 169 deaths
Annual Congo River flooding of low-lying districts
regions.region_notes: Kinshasa, one of Africa's largest cities, sits on the south bank of the Congo River â€” the world's deepest river and second largest by discharge. Rapid growth of informal settlements (cite) in flood-prone valleys and riverine plains creates extreme vulnerability. The city receives over 1,400mm of rain annually. Virtually no formal stormwater drainage exists in most of the city. The Congo basin's hydrology is highly amplified by tropical deforestation upstream.

notable_flood_events: 2005 Hurricane Katrina â€” 1,833 deaths, 80% of city flooded
2012 Hurricane Isaac â€” levee overtopping
2021 Hurricane Ida â€” renewed flood threat
regions.region_notes: New Orleans sits in a bowl-shaped depression, 50% below sea level. Katrina caused catastrophic levee failure flooding 80% of the city. Post-Katrina investment rebuilt infrastructure, but sea-level rise and subsidence continue.

notable_flood_events: 2017 Hurricane Harvey â€” 50+ inches of rain, 36 deaths, $125B damage (costliest US flood ever)
2015 Memorial Day floods
2016 Tax Day floods
regions.region_notes: Houston is the most flood-prone major US city. It sits on flat coastal prairie with heavy clay soils. Rapid suburban expansion has consumed prairies and wetlands that absorbed rainfall.

notable_flood_events: Annual valley flooding of colonias populares
2021 Tula floods â€” industrial area submerged
Historical Lake Texcoco drainage has created subsidence-driven flood paradox
regions.region_notes: Mexico City was built on a drained lake (Lake Texcoco). Groundwater extraction is causing the city to sink at up to 40cm/year in some areas â€” while paradoxically creating flooding in others.

notable_flood_events: 2017 Hurricane Irma
2022 Hurricane Ian (Fort Myers nearby)
Increasing 'sunny day flooding' from tidal sea-level rise â€” now occurring 25+ days/year
regions.region_notes: Miami is ground zero for sea-level rise in the United States. Built on porous limestone, it cannot be protected by seawalls â€” water seeps up from below.

notable_flood_events: 1998 Hurricane Mitch â€” 7,000+ deaths in Honduras, entire neighbourhoods buried in Tegucigalpa
2020 Hurricanes Eta and Iota â€” 200+ deaths
regions.region_notes: Tegucigalpa is built on steep hillsides in a valley ringed by mountains. Hurricane Mitch caused catastrophic hillside collapses that buried entire colonias.

notable_flood_events: Annual summer flash floods â€” 50-100 deaths per year on average
2010 SÃ£o Paulo floods â€” 200+ deaths
2024 Rio Grande do Sul floods â€” 150+ deaths, worst in Brazil's history (nearby state)
regions.region_notes: SÃ£o Paulo experiences extreme urban flash flooding every summer rainy season. The city sits on a plateau but has channelled and buried most of its rivers, eliminating flood absorption.

notable_flood_events: 2013 La Plata floods â€” 89 deaths
2014 Buenos Aires floods
Annual RÃ­o de la Plata storm surge flooding
regions.region_notes: Buenos Aires sits on the RÃ­o de la Plata estuary. Intense southeasterly winds (sudestadas) drive storm surges that back up rivers and flood low-lying areas.

notable_flood_events: 2017 El NiÃ±o Costero floods â€” 100 deaths, 160,000 homes damaged
Regular Huaico (debris flows) from Andes descend into Lima
regions.region_notes: Lima sits in a coastal desert but is uniquely vulnerable to El NiÃ±o-driven extreme rainfall. Huaicos â€” devastating debris flows from the Andes â€” have killed hundreds.

notable_flood_events: 1997-98 El NiÃ±o â€” massive flooding of coastal Ecuador
2008 Ecuador floods
Annual Guayas River flooding during wet season
regions.region_notes: Guayaquil sits on the Guayas River delta on Ecuador's Pacific coast. El NiÃ±o events bring catastrophic rainfall to the normally dry coast.

notable_flood_events: 2021 Amazon floods â€” worst in recorded history, 500,000 affected
2022 Amazon floods surpassed 2021 records
Annual Rio Negro flooding of waterfront communities
regions.region_notes: Manaus sits at the confluence of the Rio Negro and Amazon. Annual Amazon floods are a natural cycle, but deforestation has dramatically increased their intensity and duration.

notable_flood_events: 2011 Queensland floods â€” Brisbane River 4.46m above normal, $2.5B damage, 33 deaths
2022 Brisbane floods â€” worst since 1974, $2.5B damage
regions.region_notes: Brisbane sits on the Brisbane River and is subject to severe flooding from La NiÃ±a-driven rainfall. The 2011 and 2022 floods were separated by only 11 years, suggesting increasing frequency.

notable_flood_events: 2016 Cyclone Winston â€” strongest Southern Hemisphere cyclone on record
2012 Fiji floods
Annual cyclone season flooding
regions.region_notes: Suva and Fiji face existential climate threats. As a small island developing state, Fiji experiences intense tropical cyclones, sea-level rise, and storm surges.

notable_flood_events: 2022 PNG floods â€” 20,000 displaced
Annual flooding of Laloki and Brown river basins
2015 flash floods â€” 10 deaths in settlements
regions.region_notes: Port Moresby has a tropical monsoon climate with intense seasonal rainfall. Rapid informal urbanisation on steep slopes creates severe landslide-flood coupling risk.

notable_flood_events: 2024 UAE floods following extreme rainfall
Urban surface flooding during rare convective storms
regions.region_notes: Dubai is usually arid but highly exposed to intense short-duration rainfall because dense road and drainage systems were not designed for frequent cloudburst events.

notable_flood_events: 2009 Istanbul flash floods
Repeated Bosphorus and low-lying district stormwater flooding
regions.region_notes: Istanbul combines steep urban catchments, dense impervious surfaces, and coastal exposure along the Bosphorus and Marmara Sea. Short intense storms can overwhelm drainage channels and road underpasses.

notable_flood_events: 1910 Great Flood of Paris
2016 Seine flooding
2018 Seine flood disruption
regions.region_notes: Paris is exposed to Seine basin flooding and increasingly intense rainfall over a highly urbanized surface. Critical transport and utility assets remain vulnerable when river levels rise across the basin.

notable_flood_events: 2024 Kenya floods â€” 200+ deaths, worst in decades; El NiÃ±o-linked
2018 Mathare Valley flash floods â€” 30+ deaths
Annual flooding of Nairobi River basin informal settlements
regions.region_notes: Nairobi sits on elevated East African plateau terrain but flash floods are frequent along the Nairobi, Ngong, and Mathare river valleys. Massive informal settlements (Kibera, Mathare, Mukuru) occupy flood-prone lowlands. The 2024 El NiÃ±o-driven floods were among the worst in Kenya's history. Rapid urban expansion without commensurate drainage investment creates acute vulnerability. Climate change is intensifying East African long-rain and short-rain season extremes.

notable_flood_events: 2020 Addis Ababa flash floods â€” 50+ deaths
2023 flooding of Bole and Nifas Silk Lafto sub-cities
Annual flooding of Kebena and Akaki river valleys
regions.region_notes: Addis Ababa is Africa's highest capital at over 2,300m elevation, situated on volcanic highland terrain cut by rivers that flash-flood violently during Ethiopia's kiremt (main monsoon) season. The Kebena, Bantiyiktu, and Akaki rivers regularly overflow. Massive informal settlements on steep riverbanks face compound landslide-flood risk. Rapid densification without drainage investment is the core vulnerability driver.

notable_flood_events: 2019 Bangui floods â€” 20,000 displaced
2020 Ubangi river overflow flooding
Annual wet season flooding of riverine districts
regions.region_notes: Bangui sits on the Ubangi River (Congo border) in a region with over 1,500mm annual rainfall. The city is one of Africa's most underserved in terms of infrastructure, and virtually no formal flood management exists. Ongoing conflict further limits disaster response capacity. Low-lying riverine neighborhoods flood annually with displacement a predictable outcome.

notable_flood_events: 2022 Pearl River floods â€” Guangdong province worst flooding in decades
2020 Pearl River system flooding â€” record discharges
Annual typhoon season urban flooding
regions.region_notes: Guangzhou, capital of Guangdong province, sits at the head of the Pearl River Delta â€” one of China's most economically vital and flood-exposed regions. Typhoons tracking from the South China Sea and intense summer monsoon rainfall drive extreme flood events. The Pearl River basin receives average 1,700mm annually but can experience 3x that in typhoon years. Urban flood risk is significant despite major Chinese investment in sponge city infrastructure.

notable_flood_events: 1998 Yangtze River floods â€” 3,656 deaths across China, Wuhan severely threatened
2020 Yangtze floods â€” Wuhan at risk again, Three Gorges under extreme pressure
Annual summer Yangtze flood season
regions.region_notes: Wuhan sits at the confluence of the Yangtze and Han rivers in Hubei province. It is one of China's most flood-vulnerable major cities â€” historically averaging one major flood every 10 years. The Three Gorges Dam upstream provides flood mitigation but does not eliminate risk. Lake Dongting and Poyang systems interact with the Yangtze to create complex flood dynamics. China has invested heavily in embankments and dredging post-1998.

notable_flood_events: 2011 Mekong floods â€” catastrophic regional event, Cambodia severely affected
2020 Mekong flooding â€” extensive displacement in low-lying provinces
Annual TonlÃ© Sap lake inundation cycle
regions.region_notes: Phnom Penh sits at the confluence of the Mekong, TonlÃ© Sap, and Bassac rivers â€” one of the most complex hydrological junctions in Southeast Asia. The TonlÃ© Sap reverses flow direction seasonally, expanding from 2,500 kmÂ² to 16,000 kmÂ² during flood season. As Mekong basin countries build upstream dams, natural flood buffering is disrupted. Low-lying urban areas face seasonal inundation with increasing intensity.

notable_flood_events: 2015 Tbilisi flash floods â€” 19 deaths, zoo animals escaped into city
2019 Rustavi flood event
Recurring Vere and Mtkvari river flash flooding
regions.region_notes: Tbilisi sits in the Mtkvari (Kura) River valley, flanked by steep Caucasus foothills. The 2015 flood was a catastrophic flash flood that killed 19 people and released animals from the city zoo. Steep mountain catchments above the city channel intense precipitation into narrow urban gorges. Climate change is increasing extreme precipitation events across the Caucasus region.

notable_flood_events: 2023 Chile megadrought-flood paradox â€” sudden extreme rain after years of drought
1987 Mapocho river flooding â€” major historical event
2016 Atacama floods â€” extreme aluviones from Andes
regions.region_notes: Santiago sits in a semi-arid valley between the Andes and the Pacific Coast Range, crossed by the Mapocho River. Chile increasingly faces a drought-flood paradox: multi-year megadroughts bake soils hard, then rare but intense rainfall causes catastrophic aluviones (flash floods and debris flows) from Andean slopes. Climate change is amplifying both extremes.

notable_flood_events: 2011 Colombia floods â€” worst in 60 years (La NiÃ±a), 3M affected nationally
2017 Mocoa landslide-flood â€” 330+ deaths
Annual BogotÃ¡ River and Fucha River flooding of urban margins
regions.region_notes: BogotÃ¡ sits on the BogotÃ¡ savanna (sabana) at 2,600m elevation, making it the world's highest capital-region megacity. The BogotÃ¡ and Fucha rivers cross the city, and low-lying informal settlements (humedales areas) are flood-prone. Colombia's bimodal rainfall pattern creates two annual flood seasons. Upstream deforestation of Andean slopes amplifies sediment transport and flood intensity.

notable_flood_events: 2023 Auckland Anniversary Weekend floods â€” worst flooding in city history, 4 deaths, $1B+ damage
2023 Cyclone Gabrielle â€” national emergency, widespread flooding
2017 Edgecumbe flooding (Bay of Plenty)
regions.region_notes: Auckland sits on a narrow isthmus between two harbours. The 2023 Anniversary Weekend floods dropped a year's worth of rain in 24 hours and were followed just weeks later by Cyclone Gabrielle, creating New Zealand's costliest natural disaster sequence. Climate change is increasing extreme rainfall intensity in New Zealand's northwest flow patterns. The city's drainage infrastructure was not designed for these rainfall intensities.

notable_flood_events: 2019 Iraq floods (218 deaths, 100+ cities affected)
Historical Tigris-Euphrates flooding
Climate change intensifying extreme weather events
regions.region_notes: Baghdad straddles the Tigris river and faces threat from both river overflow and upstream dam failures in Turkey and Iran. The 2019 floods affected multiple provinces along the Tigris-Euphrates system.

notable_flood_events: 1953 North Sea flood (2,000+ deaths across Netherlands)
1995 Meuse-Rhine flooding (250,000 evacuated)
Recurring storm surge threats from North Sea
regions.region_notes: Rotterdam is Europe's largest port, built in the Rhine-Meuse delta at and below sea level. Modern flood defenses are world-leading with dykes, storm barriers, and managed retreat zones.

notable_flood_events: 2013 Central European floods (25+ deaths, Euro 12B damages)
2002 Vltava flood (17 deaths)
Medieval records document recurring Vltava inundations
regions.region_notes: Prague lies along the Vltava river. The 2002 floods inundated the metro system and historic Old Town. Climate change is intensifying extreme rainfall and snowmelt events.

live_api_integration.description: Configuration for combining static regional profiles with live weather data
live_api_integration.recommended_api: Open-Meteo (https://open-meteo.com) â€” free, open-source, no API key required

integration_pattern.step_1: Load static region profile from this file
integration_pattern.step_2: Fetch live 7-day precipitation forecast from Open-Meteo using city lat/lon
integration_pattern.step_3: Override or blend dynamic weather-driven features (MonsoonIntensity etc.)
integration_pattern.step_4: Pass combined feature vector to trained ML pipeline
integration_pattern.step_5: Output: dynamic flood risk score + risk category
